PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Jul-29-2019 Granted Per Written Order

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Jul-08-2019 Continued To Jul-29-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Karen Koulmentas in Propria Persona.
Jacqueline Johnson (Sister of Decedent) also present.

Ms. Johnson stated that she waives notice and that she has no interest in seeing the papers. The Court informed Ms. Koulmentas that generally a Fiduciary can not be self represented because they are representing other folks interest too, that probate can be very complicated and that there are very technical issues. Ms. Koulmentas stated that her goal is to do this as simple as possible, that the website is very complicated, that she is not very good as far as the legal side, that she has been diligent with the accounts, the creditors, the caregivers and the care facility, and that she prefers to do it herself. The Court stated that its preference is that Ms. Koulmentas be represented by Counsel. The Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary is continued to July 29, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for Ms. Koulmentas to talk with an Attorney or come back and explain what she has done.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Chan. Not reported.
